claim.content: Interpreting the Sommerfeld coefficient $\gamma\equiv\lim_{T\to0}C_{el}/T$ as a measure of the quasiparticle effective-mass density of states in a Fermi liquid, the observed reduction of $\gamma$ from $\sim1\ \mathrm{J\,K^{-2}\,mol^{-1}}$ in the high-field heavy-fermion state to $\simeq0.1\ \mathrm{J\,K^{-2}\,mol^{-1}}$ in the ordered state (ODS) is taken to indicate a substantial quenching of the heavy-fermion enhancement (a large decrease of the quasiparticle density of states), and other possible mechanisms that can reduce $C/T$ (for example, partial gapping of a Fermi-surface sheet, strong carrier depletion, or changes in electron-phonon coupling) are considered subdominant or insufficient to account for the magnitude and magnetic-field dependence of the observed suppression.
